title: Shinji Rei in the Scheme of Things
display_title: Shinji Rei in the Scheme of Things
wikitext:
[]: Shinji/Rei in the Scheme of Things             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Dec 05, 2009 7:20 pm This thread is probably more suited to the discussion forum, but as I wanted to get the opinions of as many people as possible, I thought I would try posting it in chit-chat. This thread is not intended to be a shipping thread, though it may border on such territory. It's also not intended to be an in depth analysis thread, though that may happen. So, what is this thread actually about? Well, my own personal position on Evangelion has for some time been that the Shinji/Rei(romantic) relationship in Evangelion is essentially not very important. My view has been that the Shinji/Asuka relationship is by far the dominant one, and I have so far been quite dismissive of Shinji/Rei in any serious capacity. In fact, I hadn't really thought it existed at all! But, I have recently begun to wonder if my position here is really justified. Is there more to Shinji/Rei than simple ambiguity or fan-service? Can the 'shipping be so easily set aside in analysis, both in regard to Shinji/Asuka, and also in its own right? My fear is that I have been too quick in passing over Shinji/Rei(romantically) as something with deeper meaning for the show. So, I'd like to hear what others think of the Shinji/Rei romantic pairing, and what bearing it has on the show overall. Do you think it exists? If so, how important is it? If not, does its potential still have effect? What impact does it have both on the plot and on other relationships in the series? Keep in mind, Shinji/Rei here refers to the romantic, not platonic pairing; though the two are no doubt related. And of course, lets try not to get bogged down in in a 'shipping squabble. I'm really interested in uncovering the true extent of this relationship in its own right.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sat Dec 05, 2009 7:25 pm I'm a bit confused about why the Shinji/Rei relationship is in the show, to be honest. It seems like an Oedipal relationship that never happened. Rei seems in place to be Jocasta and Gendo to be Laius, but it never gets to that point. I think ultimately Rei is just Shinji's spirituual guide with a more or less unrequited love for him. Their relationship isn't central to the show, but it's failry important to Rei's arc.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 05, 2009 8:03 pm In my opinion, Rei and Shinji as a romantic pairing doesn't even exist in the series as well as Rebuild. 2.0  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Dec 05, 2009 8:34 pm indeed, those are very good points personally, the only way a romantic relationship between Rei & Shinji would be able to work is that you have to RADICALLY alter Rei's character, character arc & purpose in the plot in a way that it keeps everything else fine, plot-wise, because due to certain circumstances, Rei can't really comprehend most normal human emotions SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sat Dec 05, 2009 8:35 pm ^Misato-san for me, please But this ISNT the thread for THAT

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Sat Dec 05, 2009 8:38 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: the latter especially confuses me that somehow morals don't matter if Rei/Shinji are in love with each other

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Dec 05, 2009 8:43 pm in some countries, incest is not considered immoral keep that in mind.

[]:             Quote Post by DAVEvangelion » Sat Dec 05, 2009 8:47 pm My feeling has always been that, because Rei was imparted with the soul of Lilith and cloned from Yui Ikari, she loves Shinji. Her love is maternal in the sense that Lilith is the mother of all mankind, but she does not love Shinji in the way that a romantic relationship would connote one bond of love between two people.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Sat Dec 05, 2009 8:50 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: In my opinion, Rei and Shinji as a romantic pairing doesn't even exist in the series as well as Rebuild. 2.0  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:03 pm GasmaskAvenger wrote: in some countries, incest is not considered immoral keep that in mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:04 pm DIDDY wrote: Sailor Star Dust wrote: the latter especially confuses me that somehow morals don't matter if Rei/Shinji are in love with each other

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:06 pm DIDDY wrote: GasmaskAvenger wrote: in some countries, incest is not considered immoral keep that in mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:09 pm In the original series I'd say Rei probably had feelings for Shinji but I don't think Shinji had any strong feelings for Rei. Asuka and Shinji had a stronger bond than Shinji and Rei however... Semi-Rebuild 2.0  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:22 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: In the original series I'd say Rei probably had feelings for Shinji but I don't think Shinji had any strong feelings for Rei. Asuka and Shinji had a stronger bond than Shinji and Rei however... Semi-2.0  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:35 pm SSD: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:39 pm I think the manga and Rebuild both take the Oedipal theme that remains latent in the show and run with it. The TV series itself doesn't do much with that idea, though. Sadamoto's emphasis on the Shinji/Rei relationship probably stems from the fact that he views NGE as being primarily a father/son conflict.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:43 pm @SSD: Simply put: your belief that mother/son incest is wrong skews your analysis, making you leave less room for interpretation than there actually is. (Not that I think a correct analysis would lead to the conclusion that Rei and Shinji had romantic feelings for each other.) AND ALSO (Not that I think mother/son incest is right.) ------------ Immoral relationships can (and do) occur IRL. A fortiori they can occur in fiction (where anything is possible), don't you think? The fact that Rei and Shinji's feelings would be immoral if they had romantic feelings for each other does not mean that it is impossible that they had romantic feelings for each other. On its own, this fact does not even mean it's improbable that they had feelings for each other (especially since, again, we're talking fiction, here).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:46 pm Gamer_2k4 wrote: SSD: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:50 pm I'm not entirely sure if questions of incest come particularly to the fore when discussing Shinji and Rei, except in an academic sense. Neither are explicitly aware of their "heritage", though Shinji slowly grows to suspect this. And the connection is not what would be called "standard". Rei is probably composed of PWM, and is essentially Lilith. Can the two really be said to be "related"? Sailor Star Dust wrote: Besides that, Shinji/Rei as a couple misses part of the point with the series' narrative: Mother is the first other, you leave your mother for your significant other.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sat Dec 05, 2009 9:56 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I'm not entirely sure if questions of incest come particularly to the fore when discussing Shinji and Rei, except in an academic sense. Neither are explicitly aware of their "heritage", though Shinji slowly grows to suspect this. And the connection is not what would be called "standard". Rei is probably composed of PWM, and is essentially Lilith. Can the two really be said to be "related"?
